'''Cain Robb''' (born {{birth date|2003|01|05|df=y}}) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hk}} for [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has spent time on loan at [[Salford Red Devils]] in the Super League, and at [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]], [[Swinton Lions]] and [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

==Background==
Robb was born in [[Leeds]], West Yorkshire, England.

Robb played junior rugby league for [[West Hull ARLFC]] and [[Beverley A.R.L.F.C.|Beverley Braves]]. He joined the [[Castleford Tigers]] development system at scholarship level.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=27 May 2022 |title=Boost for Castleford Tigers as youngster Cain Robb signs new deal |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/boost-for-castleford-tigers-as-youngster-cain-robb-signs-new-deal-3711560 |access-date=10 November 2024 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Castleford Tigers===
Robb made his [[Super League]] debut for [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] on 11 July 2021 against the [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-07-11 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 18-70 Salford Red Devils - Salford thrash youthful Cas side |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57781068 |access-date=2022-03-27 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He was one of five debutants, with senior players rested ahead of the [[2021 Challenge Cup final|Challenge Cup final]], and was voted Fans' Man of the Match for his performance.<ref>{{cite tweet|number=1414979828571418626|user=CTRLFC|title=Congratulations to Cain Robb who has been voted as the @HuntersHomes Fans Man of the Match for his outstanding debut performance against Salford!|author=Castleford Tigers|author-link=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> In his second senior appearance three weeks later, he was once again named Fans' Man of the Match.<ref>{{cite tweet|number=1422927807219331090|user=CTRLFC|title=Cain Robb has been voted as the @HuntersHomes Fans Man of the Match for the second time in as many games for his fearless display against Huddersfield Giants!|author=Castleford Tigers|author-link=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

Ahead of the [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|2024 season]], pundit and former Tigers director of rugby [[Jon Wells]] predicted Robb to be a rising star in Super League and likened his playing style to that of [[Daryl Clark]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Wells |first=Jon |date=15 February 2024 |title=Super League pundit predictions: Are Wigan the team to beat? Who are the underdogs? Is Jack Welsby the one to watch? |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/13044003/super-league-pundit-predictions-are-wigan-the-team-to-beat-who-are-the-underdogs-is-jack-welsby-the-one-to-watch |access-date=11 November 2024 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref> On 21 May, he signed a two-year contract extension with Castleford.<ref>{{Cite web |date=21 May 2024 |title=Castleford's Mellor and Robb sign two-year deals |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cp00l4d3ev3o |access-date=11 November 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=21 May 2024 |title='A very special player': Castleford Tigers tie down Cain Robb to continue homegrown focus |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/a-very-special-player-castleford-tigers-tie-down-cain-robb-to-continue-homegrown-focus-4637183 |access-date=11 November 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> In June, he was voted Man of the Match in consecutive weeks against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] and [[Hull KR]],<ref>{{cite tweet|number=1802703226115875027|user=CTRLFC|title=Friday's @HuntersHomes Man of the Match goes to Cain Robb!|author=Castleford Tigers|author-link=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref>{{cite tweet|number=1805299859114934547|user=CTRLFC|title=Your @HuntersHomes Man of the Match from Thursday was, of course, Cain Robb!|author=Castleford Tigers|author-link=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> and head coach [[Craig Lingard]] said, "His teammates love him because of that effort and desire. He is getting smarter in his game, he has been sensational the past few weeks."<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=20 June 2024 |title=Craig Lingard explains defeat to Hull KR and hails "sensational" Castleford Tigers star for changing his opinion |url=https://www.totalrl.com/craig-lingard-explains-defeat-to-hull-kr-and-hails-sensational-castleford-tigers-star-for-changing-his-opinion/ |access-date=11 November 2024 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> Robb suffered successive shoulder and head injuries in August to cut his season short.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Cook |first=Sam |date=13 August 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers player suffers season-ending injury in training |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/castleford-tigers-player-suffers-season-ending-injury-in-training-95907/ |access-date=11 November 2024 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref> He made 18 appearances and was noted as Castleford's standout young player.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=9 November 2024 |title=Every Super League club's breakthrough star of 2024 including England stars present and future |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/every-super-league-clubs-breakout-star-2024 |access-date=11 November 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

====Swinton Lions (loan)====
On 3 August 2023, Robb joined [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan until the end of the 2023 season.<ref>{{Cite web |date=3 Aug 2023|title=Robb joins Swinton on loan|url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8711|accessdate=21 Aug 2023|website=www.castlefordtigers.com}}</ref> Having played three games, Robb was recalled to Castleford on 4 September.<ref>{{Cite web |date=4 September 2023 |title=Robb recalled from loan |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8745 |url-status=dead |archive-url=http://web.archive.org/web/20231028175020/https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8745 |archive-date=28 October 2023 |access-date=28 April 2026 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Pattison |first=Cam |date=6 September 2023 |title=Super League club forced to draft in player from Championship club |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/super-league-club-forced-to-draft-in-player-from-championship-club-81532/ |access-date=28 April 2026 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref>

====Salford Red Devils (loan)====
On 20 June 2025, Robb made a two-week loan move to [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=20 June 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers' Cain Robb makes loan switch to Super League rivals |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-cain-robb-makes-loan-switch-to-super-league-rivals/ |access-date=28 April 2026 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> After a failed HIA suffered on debut ruled him out of the following match, his loan was extended by one further week before he returned to Castleford.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=2 July 2025 |title=Salford loan star's deal extended but second man's short-term move ends |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-loan-stars-deal-extended-but-second-mans-short-term-move-ends |access-date=28 April 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

====Batley Bulldogs (loan)====
On 31 March 2026, Robb joined the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the Championship on a one-month loan deal, marking his return from knee injury.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=31 March 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers hooker lands Championship loan switch as destination confirmed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/cain-robb-batley-loan-31-03-2026 |access-date=28 April 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite tweet |number=2039048027071991876 |user=BatleyRLFC |title=Cain Robb joins on loan! |author=Batley Bulldogs |author-link=Batley Bulldogs}}</ref> He made three appearances for Batley before his recall to Castleford on 28 April.<ref name="RLRKC" />
